justification: All standard ADR sections present (Title, Status, Context, Decision, Consequences, Alternatives Considered). Crash-recovery lock behavior explicitly described in both Rationale and Trade-offs/Risks sections. All three alternatives listed (Redis SETNX, ZooKeeper, heartbeat table) with rejection rationale. Context clearly explains the multi-node expansion problem. No invented information. ADR is fully readable as a standalone document.

observations.text: ADR is exceptionally well-structured with all required sections plus bonus sections (Rationale, Trade-offs and Risks) that add depth beyond the minimum
Crash-recovery risk is covered thoroughly: lock held until connection drop is stated, and mitigations (statement_timeout, idle_in_transaction_session_timeout) are provided
All three MJ alternatives listed in a clear comparison table with specific rejection reasons
Context section clearly explains the multi-node expansion problem and why coordination is needed
No invented information — alternatives and context stay faithful to MJ's input
Additional operational risks identified (connection pooler incompatibility, limited observability) that add genuine value without fabricating source material
The handoff brief wraps the ADR with testable acceptance criteria and domain terminology alignment, making it actionable for downstream agents
